input.prompt:
I have a .NET MAUI app with a ContentPage containing labels, buttons, and
a background color. Everything is hardcoded to light colors right now.
I want the app to automatically switch between light and dark mode based
on the device's system theme. Show me how to use AppThemeBinding to make
this page theme-aware in XAML and explain the C# alternative.

input.initialPrompt:
I have a .NET MAUI app with a ContentPage containing labels, buttons, and
a background color. Everything is hardcoded to light colors right now.
I want the app to automatically switch between light and dark mode based
on the device's system theme. Show me how to use AppThemeBinding to make
this page theme-aware in XAML and explain the C# alternative.

data.content:
## AppThemeBinding in .NET MAUI

### XAML Approach

Replace hardcoded values with `AppThemeBinding` markup extensions:

```xml
<ContentPage xmlns="http://schemas.microsoft.com/dotnet/2021/maui"
             xmlns:x="http://schemas.microsoft.com/winfx/2009/xaml"
             x:Class="MyApp.MainPage"
             BackgroundColor="{AppThemeBinding Light=#FFFFFF, Dark=#1E1E1E}">

    <VerticalStackLayout Padding="20" Spacing="16">

        <Label Text="Welcome"
               FontSize="28"
               TextColor="{AppThemeBinding Light=#000000, Dark=#E0E0E0}" />

        <Label Text="This page adapts to your system theme."
               FontSize="16"
               TextColor="{AppThemeBinding Light=#444444, Dark=#BBBBBB}" />

        <Button Text="Get Started"
                BackgroundColor="{AppThemeBinding Light=#6200EE, Dark=#BB86FC}"
                TextColor="{AppThemeBinding Light=#FFFFFF, Dark=#000000}" />

    </VerticalStackLayout>
</ContentPage>
```

Every `AppThemeBinding` takes a **Light** and **Dark** value. MAUI watches `Application.Current.RequestedTheme` and swaps them automatically — no event wiring needed.

### Cleaner: Use Resource Dictionaries

Avoid repeating colors everywhere by defining them once in `App.xaml`:

```xml
<!-- App.xaml -->
<Application.Resources>
    <Color x:Key="PageBg">{AppThemeBinding Light=#FFFFFF, Dark=#1E1E1E}</Color>  <!-- ❌ won't work here -->
</Application.Resources>
```

> `AppThemeBinding` doesn't work directly on `<Color>` resources. Use a `Style` instead:

```xml
<Application.Resources>
    <Style TargetType="ContentPage" ApplyToDerivedTypes="True">
        <Setter Property="BackgroundColor"
                Value="{AppThemeBinding Light=#FFFFFF, Dark=#1E1E1E}" />
    </Style>
    <Style x:Key="HeadingLabel" TargetType="Label">
        <Setter Property="TextColor"
                Value="{AppThemeBinding Light=#000000, Dark=#E0E0E0}" />
    </Style>
</Application.Resources>
```

Then your page becomes simply:

```xml
<Label Text="Welcome" Style="{StaticResource HeadingLabel}" />
```

### C# Code-Behind Alternative

Use `SetAppTheme` or `SetAppThemeColor` extension methods:

```csharp
// In your page constructor or OnAppearing
var label = new Label { Text = "Welcome", FontSize = 28 };
label.SetAppThemeColor(Label.TextColorProperty, Colors.Black, Colors.White);
//                                               ↑ light      ↑ dark

var button = new Button { Text = "Get Started" };
button.SetAppThemeColor(Button.BackgroundColorProperty,
    Color.FromArgb("#6200EE"),   // light
    Color.FromArgb("#BB86FC"));  // dark

// For non-color properties use SetAppTheme<T>:
label.SetAppTheme<string>(Label.TextProperty, "Light Mode", "Dark Mode");
```

### How It Works

| Concept | Detail |
|---|---|
| **Source** | Reads `Application.Current.RequestedTheme` (`Light` / `Dark`) |
| **Reactive** | Automatically updates when the user toggles the OS theme |
| **Override** | Set `Application.Current.UserAppTheme = AppTheme.Dark` to force a theme |
| **Reset** | Set `UserAppTheme = AppTheme.Unspecified` to follow the system again |

No platform-specific code is required — MAUI handles iOS, Android, Windows, and macOS uniformly.
data.reasoningText: The user is asking for a tutorial/explanation on how to use `AppThemeBinding` in .NET MAUI to support light/dark mode theming. This is a knowledge question, not a codebase task. I'll provide a concise, practical example.
